two-tier-hierarchy-of-independent-vs-dep.name: Two-tier hierarchy of independent vs. dependent reality
two-tier-hierarchy-of-independent-vs-dep.definition: Whether the school carves reality into one independent and one or more dependent strata, e.g. Madhva's svatantra/paratantra and Advaita's pāramārthika/vyāvahārika.

number-of-fundamental-existents.name: Number of fundamental existents
number-of-fundamental-existents.definition: Whether ultimate reality counts as one (Brahman alone), two (Brahman + souls), three (Brahman + souls + world), or more eternally distinct categories.

status-of-the-world.name: Status of the world
status-of-the-world.definition: Whether the world is real, mithyā (sad-asad-vilakṣaṇa), an avikṛta-pariṇāma of Brahman, an aupādhika appearance, or acintya-bhedābhinna with Brahman.

status-of-individual-selves.name: Status of individual selves
status-of-individual-selves.definition: Whether jīvas are numerically identical with Brahman, merely apparently distinct, eternally distinct aṃśas, bimba-pratibimba images, or acintya-bhedābhinna parts.

modal-status-of-difference-itself.name: Modal status of difference itself
modal-status-of-difference-itself.definition: Whether bheda is a positive entity (Madhva's pañca-bheda as svarūpa), a vyāvṛtti / mutual exclusion, or an aupādhika superimposition.

jiva-brahman-relation.name: Jīva–Brahman relation
jiva-brahman-relation.definition: Specific structural relation posited between the individual self and Brahman: identity, aṃśa, pratibimba, śarīra-śarīri, acintya-bhedābheda, abhinna-but-distinct.

three-types-of-difference.name: Three types of difference (svagata, sajātīya, vijātīya)
three-types-of-difference.definition: Which of the three classical kinds of difference — internal-to-substrate, between-things-of-same-kind, between-things-of-different-kind — Brahman is taken to admit or exclude.

ontological-tier-structure.name: Ontological tier-structure
ontological-tier-structure.definition: Whether the school admits multiple ontological tiers (e.g., pāramārthika/vyāvahārika/prātibhāsika in Advaita), and what work each tier does.

type-of-cause.name: Type of causation
type-of-cause.definition: Vivarta-vāda (apparent transformation), pariṇāma-vāda (real transformation), satkārya / asatkārya, ārambha-vāda (origination), and avikāra-pariṇāma (change-without-change).

causation-satkarya-vs-asatkarya.name: Satkārya vs. asatkārya
causation-satkarya-vs-asatkarya.definition: Whether the effect pre-exists in its cause (satkārya, accepted by Sāṅkhya/Vedānta) or is a fresh production from non-existence (asatkārya, Nyāya-Vaiśeṣika).

where-causal-power-locates.name: Where causal power locates
where-causal-power-locates.definition: Whether causal power resides in Brahman alone, in māyā / prakṛti, in Lakṣmī as cit-śakti, in Śiva's svātantrya, or distributively across reals.

upadana-vs-nimitta-cause.name: Upādāna vs. nimitta cause
upadana-vs-nimitta-cause.definition: Whether Brahman serves as both material (upādāna) and efficient (nimitta) cause, or only one, with the other located elsewhere.

locus-of-avidya.name: Locus of avidyā
locus-of-avidya.definition: Whether avidyā is located in Brahman (Vivaraṇa), in the jīva (Bhāmatī), in both, or denied as a real third entity (Madhva's polemic).

status-of-vyavaharika.name: Status of vyāvahārika
status-of-vyavaharika.definition: Whether the empirical-transactional level is a metaphysically substantive sub-tier of reality or a merely pragmatic rubric, and how it relates to pāramārthika and prātibhāsika.

number-and-status-of-pramanas.name: Number and status of pramāṇas
number-and-status-of-pramanas.definition: Whether the school counts three (Advaita classical), six (Bhāṭṭa Mīmāṃsā / Advaita), or some other set as autonomous instruments of knowledge.

status-of-sruti.name: Status of śabda / śruti
status-of-sruti.definition: Whether scriptural testimony is self-validating, validated by something else, coordinate with or superior to perception and inference.

status-of-anubhava.name: Status of anubhava (immediate experience)
status-of-anubhava.definition: Whether immediate experience confirms testimony, functions as an independent pramāṇa, or is itself an object reached via testimony.

theory-of-error.name: Theory of error
theory-of-error.definition: Anyathā-khyāti (Nyāya), anirvacanīya-khyāti (Advaita), akhyāti (Mīmāṃsā), or yathārtha-khyāti (Madhva's all-cognitions-true thesis).

saving-cognition-status.name: Saving-cognition status
saving-cognition-status.definition: Whether the cognition that liberates is jñāna, bhāvanā, upāsanā, bhakti, prapatti, or the recognition (pratyabhijñā) of one's own ever-pure nature.

identity-of-liberated-state.name: Identity of the liberated state
identity-of-liberated-state.definition: Whether mokṣa is ekatva (identity), sāyujya (communion), sālokya (co-residence), sārūpya (same-form), sārṣṭi (same-power), or graded krama-mukti.

moksa-as-isolation-vs-union.name: Mokṣa as isolation vs. union
moksa-as-isolation-vs-union.definition: Whether liberation is isolation of the self from world-bondage (kaivalya-leaning) or eternal union/communion with the personal Lord (sāyujya-leaning).

persistence-of-jiva-individuality-post-m.name: Persistence of jīva-individuality post-mukti
persistence-of-jiva-individuality-post-m.definition: Whether jīva-individuality is annihilated, preserved with full intrinsic ānanda, or preserved as an aṃśa within Brahman's body.

jivanmukti-possibility.name: Possibility of jīvanmukti
jivanmukti-possibility.definition: Whether liberation is achievable while embodied (yes for Advaita; denied by strict Madhva; nuanced for others).

effective-means-of-moksa.name: Effective means of mokṣa
effective-means-of-moksa.definition: Whether liberation is achieved by jñāna alone, jñāna + karma, bhakti, prapatti, līlā-recognition, or some specified combination.

role-of-grace-vs-effort.name: Role of grace vs. effort
role-of-grace-vs-effort.definition: The relative weight of divine kṛpā and human sādhana, including the Vaḍakalai monkey-hold and Tenkalai cat-hold positions on prapatti.

role-of-bhakti.name: Role of bhakti
role-of-bhakti.definition: Whether bhakti is a preliminary discipline, the saving cognition itself, superior to jñāna, or identical to jñāna under another aspect.

status-of-karma.name: Status of karma
status-of-karma.definition: Whether ritual / ethical action is purificatory, to be abandoned, to be performed niṣkāma, or to be performed as pūjā.

status-of-dharma.name: Status of dharma
status-of-dharma.definition: Whether dharma is socially-instrumental, cosmologically-real, or a lakṣaṇā (indicator) of liberation.

status-of-personal-lord.name: Status of the personal Lord (Iśvara)
status-of-personal-lord.definition: Whether the personal Lord is provisional (sopādhika), ultimate, identical to Brahman, or in some sense superior to nirguṇa-Brahman.

surrender-prapatti.name: Surrender (prapatti / śaraṇāgati / ātma-nivedana)
surrender-prapatti.definition: Whether surrender is supplementary to other paths (Vaḍakalai) or itself sufficient and total (Tenkalai); whether it issues from poverty or fullness.

mithya-vs-asat.name: Mithyā vs. asat
mithya-vs-asat.definition: Whether mithyā collapses to non-existence (the Madhva polemic charge) or names sad-asad-vilakṣaṇa, neither sat nor asat (Advaita self-interpretation).

acintya-modal-operator.name: Acintya as modal operator
acintya-modal-operator.definition: Whether 'inconceivable' (acintya) functions as a serious modal qualifier on bhedābheda (Caitanya / Jīva) rather than as a confession of vagueness.

sapta-vidha-anupapatti.name: Sapta-vidhā-anupapatti
sapta-vidha-anupapatti.definition: Rāmānuja's seven untenabilities of avidyā, the Viśiṣṭādvaitin core polemic against Advaitic māyā.

sarira-sariri-bhava.name: Śarīra-śarīri-bhāva
sarira-sariri-bhava.definition: The body-soul relation Rāmānuja takes between Brahman and the totality of cit-acit modes; the Viśiṣṭādvaitin alternative to Advaitic identity.

svatantra-vs-paratantra.name: Svatantra vs. paratantra
svatantra-vs-paratantra.definition: Madhva's structuring distinction between the one independent reality (Viṣṇu) and all dependent reals; structurally compared with Advaita's pāramārthika/vyāvahārika.

panca-bheda.name: Pañca-bheda
panca-bheda.definition: Madhva's five eternal distinctions (Viṣṇu–jīva, Viṣṇu–jaḍa, jīva–jīva, jīva–jaḍa, jaḍa–jaḍa) treated as ontologically real.

bimba-pratibimba.name: Bimba-pratibimba
bimba-pratibimba.definition: The image-and-prototype relation Madhva takes between Viṣṇu and jīvas: structurally similar yet really distinct.

ajati-vada.name: Ajāti-vāda
ajati-vada.definition: Gauḍapāda's non-origination thesis: nothing whatsoever ever comes into being; the deepest root of Advaita.

vivarta-vs-parinama.name: Vivarta vs. pariṇāma
vivarta-vs-parinama.definition: Whether the world is an apparent transformation (vivarta) of Brahman as in Advaita, or a real transformation (pariṇāma) of Brahman or its śakti.

avikrta-parinama.name: Avikṛta-pariṇāma
avikrta-parinama.definition: Vallabha's thesis that Brahman undergoes real manifestation as the world without intrinsic alteration; the Śuddhādvaita alternative to vivarta.

svatantrya-divine-self-veiling.name: Svātantrya / divine self-veiling
svatantrya-divine-self-veiling.definition: The Trika thesis that the manifest world is the absolute's sovereign free self-presentation/self-veiling; the comparator-axis for Vivaraṇa Brahman-as-locus-of-avidyā.

rasa-as-saving-aesthesis.name: Rasa as saving aesthesis
rasa-as-saving-aesthesis.definition: Whether aesthetic disinterested experience (rasa) is itself a path or analog of liberating cognition (Abhinavagupta, Rūpa Gosvāmī).

school-as-bhasya-vs-school-as-living-tra.name: School-as-bhāṣya vs. school-as-living-tradition
school-as-bhasya-vs-school-as-living-tra.definition: Whether a school is identified by its securely-authored bhāṣya corpus or by its living tradition including school-ascribed stotras and devotional practice.
